things.name: Persepolis
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage ceremonial capital of Achaemenid Persia with vast columned halls

things.name: Nasir al-Mulk Mosque (Pink Mosque)
things.description: 19th-century mosque where stained-glass windows create a kaleidoscopic prayer hall at dawn

things.name: Tomb of Hafez
things.description: Garden mausoleum of Persia's greatest lyric poet, where Shirazis gather to recite his verse

things.name: Naqsh-e Rostam
things.description: Achaemenid royal necropolis with cross-shaped tombs of Darius and Xerxes carved into cliffs

things.name: Eram Garden
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage Persian garden with a 19th-century royal pavilion and cypress trees

things.name: Shah Cheragh Shrine
things.description: Spectacular mirror-mosaic mausoleum, one of Iran's most important Shia pilgrimage sites

things.name: Vakil Bazaar
things.description: 18th-century Zand bazaar with vaulted brick ceilings, carpet dealers and spice merchants

things.name: Tomb of Saadi
things.description: Garden mausoleum of Saadi Shirazi, author of the Gulistan and one of Iran's greatest poets

things.name: Vakil Mosque
things.description: Zand-dynasty mosque with 48 spiral-fluted columns carved from single pieces of marble

things.name: Pasargadae
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage tomb of Cyrus the Great - founder of the Achaemenid Persian Empire
